methods.description: Process for measuring Total Organic Carbon (TOC) and total sulfur contents in rock samples using a LECO CS-744 analyzer.

steps.description: The samples were crushed to 100 mesh for TOC measurements in a LECO CS-744 analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: crush samples to 100 mesh for TOC measurement

steps.description: The crushed samples were first pretreated with 5% hydrochloric acid and then burned in a high-temperature oxygen flow to generate oxides.
steps.searchable_summary: pretreat samples with HCl and burn in oxygen flow

steps.description: The TOC and total sulfur contents were calculated using the generated carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide with standard deviations within 0.1%.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate TOC and sulfur content from CO2 and SO2

methods.description: Procedure for evaluating the hydrocarbon potential of rock samples using Rock−Eval pyrolysis.

steps.description: Rock−Eval pyrolysis was performed using an OGE-II oil and gas evaluation workstation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Rock−Eval pyrolysis using OGE-II workstation

steps.description: The crushed samples were initially heated to 300°C in an inert atmosphere, held at the temperature of 300°C for 3 min to generate free hydrocarbon (S1).
steps.searchable_summary: heat samples to 300°C to generate S1

steps.description: Then heated to 600°C at a rate of 25°C/min to generate the pyrolysis hydrocarbon (S2).
steps.searchable_summary: heat samples to 600°C to generate S2

steps.description: The hydrogen index (HI) was calculated by normalizing S2 to the TOC.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate hydrogen index by normalizing S2 to TOC

steps.description: Tmax was defined as the temperature corresponding to the maximum generation rate of hydrocarbons in the kerogen cracking process.
steps.searchable_summary: define Tmax as temperature of max hydrocarbon generation
